title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has sparked both excitement and confusion within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. This deal marks a significant transaction in the satellite manufacturing and asset-financing sector.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract to supply a satellite bus for a Japanese technology demonstration mission. The deal marks Apex's expansion into international commercial satellite contracts.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract with the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic flight missions over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's expanding role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command is conducting a classified wargame involving 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations and integration between military and commercial space sectors.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' today. The launch can be watched live.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: Two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes will be available on BBC iPlayer in the UK. NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out the Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of its planned April 1 launch. The event will be broadcast live.

title: 'At the edge of what we thought possible': Astronomers find extremely rare star from ancient universe
summary: Astronomers discovered an extremely rare, iron-deficient second-generation star from the ancient universe, offering insights into how early stars enriched subsequent generations.

title: Astronauts complete prep for new ISS solar array on 1st NASA spacewalk in 10 months
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Chris Williams conducted a spacewalk to prepare for the installation of a new solar array on the International Space Station. This was the first NASA spacewalk in 10 months.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia experienced significant rainfall in early 2026, turning the desert landscape green. This unusual weather event transformed the region's typically arid environment.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission involves detailed geological analysis led by Catherine O'Connell-Cooper from the University of New Brunswick.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon volcano in the Philippines on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. PHIVOLCS reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds during the event.

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in technical processes and tools through the program.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The news lists various upcoming space industry events in 2026, including gatherings organized by organizations like MSBR, SIA, and NASA, as well as conferences focused on Artemis suppliers and space policy.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship program ahead of Flight 12.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of space launches is scheduled with nine launches planned, including missions by Falcon 9, Spectrum, and Electron rockets. The launches are expected to take place from various locations worldwide.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin introduced NEO Hunter, a hybrid planetary defense concept aimed at protecting Earth from near-Earth objects. The mission concept highlights Blue Origin's expanding role in space safety and exploration.

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for Moon and Mars missions at its Starfactory facility, transitioning from steel production to Starship development. The company's ambitious goals are highlighted in a recent update from NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: Following the early departure of Crew-11 due to a medical concern, new crew arrivals and scientific activities highlighted February aboard the International Space Station (ISS).

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet celebrating 50 years of achievements in the space industry. The publication highlights 50 significant milestones from the agency's history.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission is undergoing space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator to prepare for its exoplanet-hunting mission. The satellite, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is set for a January 2027 launch aboard an Ariane 6 rocket.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA is set to release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA tender actions.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its SR75 suborbital rocket, with the second flight scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first contract in Japan with NEC Corporation to purchase an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission next year. This deal marks a significant milestone for Apex in expanding its presence in the Japanese space market.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: United Launch Alliance is accelerating modifications to the Vulcan rocket to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, aiming for a launch this summer after addressing a previous anomaly.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce reliance on satellite infrastructure. The investment highlights the growing interest in alternatives to traditional satellite-dependent systems.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a deal with South Korea's national security sector, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and South Korea's space industry.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has submitted a new filing to the FCC asserting that its SkyTerra Next proposal, which involves hosting an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, will not cause interference. The proposal is designed to operate within Ligado's existing L-band coordination framework.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly supply solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea's sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) national security constellation. The collaboration marks a significant step in enhancing South Korea's space capabilities.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the critical importance of satellite communication (Satcom) terminal survivability in contested environments for U.S. military operations. It highlights the need for beyond-line-of-sight connectivity for command and control, mission execution, and safety-of-life communications.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic unveiled plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' satellites, which will offer one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The new constellation aims to enhance Earth observation services.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 introduces nine new speakers, expanding its program with a focus on military space (GovMilSpace) for its second year. The event aims to gather key satellite industry leaders and military stakeholders.
